description: Letter from Bū l-Munā b. ʿImrān al-ʿA[sqalā?]nī to Abū l-Ḥasan b. Saʿīd al-Ṣārifī Ibn al-Maṣmūdī. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: ca. 1100 CE. The addressee may be called (in the Arabic script) "the doctor of the square' (ṭabīb al-murabbaʿa). Discussing business matters. Mentions a youth Bukhūr(?) b. Nuṣayr from Sanhūr (Egypt) as an agent. The sender is in the service of Abū l-Munajjā (the digger of the canal named after him). He has many requests and in particular wishes to be relieved from his post and get the arrears of his salary. The sender has enclosed two other letters from himself for other recipients, and a letter from the cantor Abū l-Ḥasan Ibn al-Jāzfīnī to be delivered to the house of the late Nahray (b. Nissim?), with condolences on the death of a son. (Information in part from Goitein's index card and from the Halper Catalogue.)
